_comment: Mock DataHub metadata graph. Shaped like real DataHub entities (urns, datasets, schemaFields, upstream lineage, glossary terms, ownership) so this file can be swapped for a live DataHub GMS instance with no code changes beyond catalog/datahub_client.py. See README.md 'Using a real DataHub instance'.

datasets.name: customers
datasets.description: One row per registered customer. Source of truth for customer identity and region.

schemaFields.name: signup_date
schemaFields.description: Date the customer account was created.

schemaFields.name: region
schemaFields.description: Sales region: Northeast, Midwest, South, or West.

datasets.name: products
datasets.description: Product catalog with current unit price and category.

schemaFields.name: category
schemaFields.description: One of: Electronics, Home & Kitchen, Sports, Books, Toys.

datasets.name: orders
datasets.description: One row per customer order (header level). Does not contain line-item detail - see order_items.

datasets.name: order_items
datasets.description: Line-item detail for each order: which product, how many, at what price. Revenue must be computed as quantity * unit_price and summed - there is no pre-aggregated revenue column.

schemaFields.name: quantity
schemaFields.description: Units purchased in this line item.

schemaFields.name: unit_price
schemaFields.description: Price at time of purchase (may differ from current products.unit_price).

datasets.name: monthly_revenue_report
datasets.description: Derived reporting view: total revenue and order count per month per category. Rebuilt nightly from order_items joined to products and orders.

glossaryTerms.name: Email (PII)
glossaryTerms.description: Personally identifiable contact information. Handle per data-privacy policy.

glossaryTerms.name: Customer
glossaryTerms.description: An individual who has registered an account.

glossaryTerms.name: Order
glossaryTerms.description: A customer's request to purchase one or more products.

glossaryTerms.name: Revenue
glossaryTerms.description: Money earned from completed sales, computed as quantity * unit_price, summed across order_items.
